meaning: n.不满,不平,令人不满的事物
examples: Many people have expressed their dissatisfaction with the arrangement.
She has already expressed her dissatisfaction with this aspect of the policy.
Dissatisfaction among the managers soon permeated down to members of the workforce.
Mr Obama prevailed despite lingering dissatisfaction with the economy and a hard-fought challenge by Mr Romney.
Power says that baggage fees continue to be a major source of passenger dissatisfaction.
